steps.title: Identify the data pattern
steps.content: Dark irises make pupils appear large; light irises make them appear small. The table shows dogs with dark irises (images 11, 3) scored higher on positive traits: friendly ratings of 3.18–3.88 versus 1.61–2.08 for light-iris dogs, with similar increases across keep and interact measures.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the answer
steps.content: C states that humans responded more positively to large-appearing pupils in dogs. The table confirms this: friendly ratings jumped from 1.61–2.08 (light iris) to 3.18–3.88 (dark iris), with parallel increases across keep and interact measures. A reverses this relationship, B denies any pattern, and D tests a distinction (actual vs. apparent pupil size) the study never makes. This pattern demonstrates that humans transfer their innate preference for dilated pupils to dogs. That matches choice C — C is correct.
